title: Maze Product Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Python Build Room
summary: A museum puzzle team incrementally builds player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter from original seeded fixtures while retaining earlier Python, graph, determinism, accessibility, testing, performance, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Define player, task, puzzle rules, success, failure, session length, and observable value before selecting algorithms.
Inspect revision, status, environment, dependencies, licenses, existing tests, and recovery anchors before changing the project.
Record keyboard, pointer, vision, color, motion, timing, cognition, text, and assistive-technology needs as design inputs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Player Outcome
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop puzzle team handles case 5535 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the maze charter on player value, repository evidence, accessibility, and honest native limits; the prior good state must survive failure; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Player Outcome.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define player, task, puzzle rules, success, failure, session length, and observable value before selecting algorithms. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Player Outcome, keep Player Outcome connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

This does not satisfy Player Outcome for player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Scope Transfer
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-scope-transfer-1c9kxv" that uses case 3571 to gate the maze charter on player value, repository evidence, accessibility, and honest native limits. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Scope Transfer.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-scope-transfer-1c9kxv
# Competency: maze-scope-transfer.
# Case 3571: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it.
def evidence_maze_scope_transfer_1c9kxv(player_task: str = "compare-solvers", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, native_limits_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1c9kxv = "1c9kxv-vxk9c1-3571"
    assert evidence_variant_1c9kxv
    evidence_axes_1c9kxv = (
        "grid1c9kxv",
        "topology3571",
        "searchvxk9c1",
        "event1c9kxv3571",
        "access35711c9kxv",
        "recoveryvxk9c13571",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1c9kxv)) == 6
    if not player_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not native_limits_named:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "native-overclaim"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-maze-charter"
